body.title: test_frame_push_does_not_flood_other_twm_categories deterministic fail — self.capabilities leakage
body.result: Investigated: test passes in full file (9/9), fails in isolation — classic shared-TWM-state, already covered by T-test-ordering-flakes and T-test-postgres-schema. NOT a real regression. More interestingly: pe_chain preflight runs 'pytest tests/ -x -q' with 120s timeout but 3988 tests can't fit — preflight likely always TIMES OUT and misreads that as failure. Filed T-pe-chain-preflight-timeout-misdiagnosis as the real issue.
body.description:
tests/test_pr_load_as_primary_attractor.py::test_frame_push_does_not_flood_other_twm_categories fails deterministically. Snapshot diff shows `_push_relationship_frame` leaks observations to other categories: +1 `self.capabilities` and +1 `observation`. Test asserts non-frame categories unchanged after frame push.

Likely cause: CapabilityAwareness push_source (push_sources.py:2576) or a side-effect call chain during `_push_relationship_frame` (main.py:2166) — maybe via `cortex.get(facia_id)` activation logging or `pr_touch()`. This is the pe_chain pre-flight blocker Igor has been hitting all afternoon.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/main.py:2166 (_push_relationship_frame), wild_igor/igor/cognition/push_sources.py:2576 (CapabilityAwareness), tests/test_pr_load_as_primary_attractor.py.
**Design rules:** test-plan-or-why-not — test already exists, just needs to pass again.
**Scope boundary:** IN — find the leakage path + fix so the non-flood test passes. OUT — redesigning CapabilityAwareness scheduling, refactoring frame push.
**Test plan:** existing test_frame_push_does_not_flood_other_twm_categories passes; no new regressions.
